decision_point: In an adult with an acute Rockwood grade I-II acromioclavicular joint sprain, is nonoperative management or surgical stabilization preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with an acute Rockwood grade I-II acromioclavicular joint sprain, is nonoperative management or surgical stabilization preferred?
Options:
  1. Nonoperative management
  2. Surgical stabilization
label: settled
label_rationale: Low-grade AC sprains reliably recover with sling and rehabilitation; surgical stabilization is reserved for high-grade injuries, so nonoperative care is settled for the typical patient.
